---
title: "Vesper Peak - Ragged Edge - 2017"
tripPlan: ""
---

Gorgeous views and a great climb!

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21740765_10214426013466404_703195198429727251_o-1024x462.jpg)

## Resources

- [Steph Abegg](http://www.stephabegg.com/home/tripreports/washington/northcascades/vesper3)

## Gear

- 60m rope
- Doubles of 0.3 - 1, singles of 2, 3
- Small nuts
- 12 quickdraws

## Climb

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21740903_10214426012106370_8696928265121360597_o-772x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.292857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:475,&quot;y&quot;:573,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;Correct crossing\n(red line)&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

![OLYMPUS DIGITAL CAMERA](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/RE_Topo21-772x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 1

I believe this photo is actually only halfway up P1, and that's where we went left off of both of the official routes...

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/27579333280_511e4261b9_b1-1024x768.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.4628571428571429,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:762,&quot;y&quot;:143,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;P2 goes over the roof on\nthe right and then back\nleft onto the face above&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.4628571428571429,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:50,&quot;y&quot;:113,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;I think this is where\nwe went left...&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 2

The following picture is the INCORRECT pitch 2 of our previous trip

![Alexis at anchor #1, we went up that slanted crack](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21728700_10214426006426228_4514736589168827609_o-1024x683.jpg)

## Pitch 3

![An old piton on Pitch 3 of the route. This pitch goes up the seam in the photo, and there are enough features to make it 5.7. -Steph Abegg](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/27822784956_e60d895f03_b1-1024x768.jpg)

## Pitch 4

![The zig zag cracks (5.7) of Pitch 4.](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/27579332540_a6481589e9_b1-768x1024.jpg)

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/27822784066_04b19bcc0b_b1-1024x768.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.4628571428571429,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:410,&quot;y&quot;:162,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;Top of P4\nanchor&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.4628571428571429,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:570,&quot;y&quot;:169},{&quot;x&quot;:623,&quot;y&quot;:158},{&quot;x&quot;:682,&quot;y&quot;:159}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.4628571428571429,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:581,&quot;y&quot;:137,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;P5&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 6

Second to last pitch

- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21688146_10214426012026368_2458600860079125341_o-1024x683.jpg) Alexis over at anchor #6, second to last pitch. It does quite a bit of traversing!
- ![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21688075_10214426009626308_2399381760872806715_o-1024x683.jpg) Alexis leaving anchor #6 and climbing the second to last pitch. I really did a lot of zig zagging protecting that route. Didn't want to take a fall there since there's an edge beneath it all that you would fall over.

## Pitch 7

![,From the top, the final last section!](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2019/08/21740830_10214426013746411_7391383789814453542_o-935x1024.jpg)
